Explanation: Using selectFrom() fetches all columns from the table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider explicitly selecting only the columns needed for the operation.

Using selectFrom() in getAll() fetches all columns from the table for every record, which can be inefficient especially for large tables. Consider selecting only the specific columns required for the use case.

Using LIKE with wildcards on both sides for searching first names performs a full table scan and cannot use indexes efficiently. Consider using a full-text search index or a dedicated search engine like Elasticsearch for text searching.

Using LIKE with wildcards on both sides for searching last names performs a full table scan and cannot use indexes efficiently. Consider using a full-text search index or a dedicated search engine like Elasticsearch for text searching.

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from the table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and hurt performance. Explicitly list only the required columns, e.g., .select(personTab.PERSON_ID, personTab.FIRST_NAME, personTab.LAST_NAME).
